intitule: Unless and Other If Substitutes: Controlled Practice

enonce.texte:
Read each sentence carefully. Choose the correct option to complete the conditional sentences using unless or inverted structures.

Consigne : Remplacez 'if...not' par 'unless' ou utilisez l'inversion quand c'est possible.

questions.texte: You will not pass the Probatoire examination ______ you study the irregular verbs.
______ the government invest in education, the country will face a skills shortage.
Identify the correct transformation of the following sentence into an inversion: 'If I were the principal, I would ban phones in class.'
questions.reponse: Were I the principal, I would ban phones in class.

questions.texte: Complete the sentence: 'She won't attend the graduation ceremony ______ she receives a scholarship.'
Rewrite using 'unless': 'If you don't pay your tuition fees on time, you will not sit for the exams.'
questions.reponse: Unless you pay your tuition fees on time, you will not sit for the exams.

enonce.texte: Read the text about a student in Yaoundé and answer the questions. Apply Bloom's taxonomy (Analyze, Evaluate, Create) to use 'unless' and inversions appropriately.

questions.texte: Analyze the situation: 'If the government does not provide more textbooks, the students' performance will drop.' Transform this sentence using 'unless'.
questions.reponse: Unless the government provides more textbooks, the students' performance will drop.

questions.texte: Evaluate the advice given to a student: 'If you should need help with conditional clauses, consult the librarian.' Create a more formal version by removing 'if'.
questions.reponse: Should you need help with conditional clauses, consult the librarian.

questions.texte: Create a piece of advice for a friend who wants to improve his English but refuses to read novels. Start with 'Unless'.
questions.reponse: Unless you start reading English novels, you won't improve your vocabulary significantly. (Accept any logical advice using 'unless')

questions.texte: The teacher says: 'If it rains, the sports competition is cancelled.' However, the students hope it doesn't rain. Express this situation using an inversion to emphasize the condition.
questions.reponse: Should it rain, the sports competition will be cancelled.

questions.texte: Context: A student in Buea is afraid of speaking English in public. Justify why using 'Unless' is more emphatic than 'If...not' to encourage him to practice.
questions.reponse: 'Unless' creates a stronger, more urgent condition. Saying 'Unless you practice, you won't improve' sounds more like a final warning or a strict boundary than 'If you don't practice, you won't improve', which is more neutral.

intitule: Reading Comprehension: Conditional Inversions in Formal Writing

enonce.texte:
Read the excerpt from a speech given by a school principal in Douala. Then answer the questions based on the use of 'unless' and inversions.

Text: 'To the graduating class, remember: Were you to neglect your studies now, you would regret it later. Unless you cultivate discipline, success will remain a mirage. Should you face challenges, remember that resilience is key. Under no circumstances should you give up on your dreams.'

questions.texte: Identify the inversion in the text and rewrite it in a standard 'if' form.
questions.reponse: Inversion: 'Were you to neglect your studies now' -> Standard: 'If you neglected your studies now' OR 'If you were to neglect your studies now'.

questions.texte: Analyze the difference in meaning between 'Unless you cultivate discipline' and 'If you don't cultivate discipline' in this specific context.
questions.reponse: In this formal speech, 'Unless' introduces the only condition that guarantees failure. It implies that discipline is the *single essential* requirement. 'If you don't' might imply there are other reasons for failure, while 'Unless' focuses specifically on discipline as the barrier.

questions.texte: The principal says 'Under no circumstances should you give up.' Create a similar sentence using inversion to emphasize the importance of submitting assignments on time.
questions.reponse: Under no circumstances should you submit your assignments late. (Accept variations like: 'On no condition should you miss the deadline.')
